diagrams.title: DFS Tree with Discovery and Low Times
diagrams.description: A sample graph with DFS tree showing disc and low values at each vertex, demonstrating articulation point identification.
elements: Graph with 6 vertices forming cycle with chord
DFS tree edges and back edges labeled
disc[u] and low[u] at each vertex
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how low values are computed and how they identify articulation points

diagrams.title: Biconnected Component Decomposition
diagrams.description: A graph decomposed into its biconnected components, showing articulation points as connection points.
elements: Original graph with 2 cycles sharing a vertex
diagrams.purpose: Understand that biconnected components are maximal subgraphs connected at articulation points

diagrams.title: Bridge vs Non-Bridge Edge
diagrams.description: Comparison showing bridge edge (removing disconnects) vs non-bridge edge (cycle preserves connectivity).
elements: Left: tree edge that is a bridge
Right: edge in cycle that is not a bridge
diagrams.purpose: Distinguish between bridge and non-bridge edges using low values
